item.title: UK regulator plans to dilute capital rules for investment banks’ trading activities
item.description: PRA says softening Basel regulations will keep Britain aligned with US and EU

item.title: KPMG Australia accused of ‘culture of fear, retribution’ in bruising hearing
item.description: Former head of audit admits to senators the firm accessed whistleblower’s laptop

item.title: FirstFT: Labour’s Andy Burnham wins crucial UK by-election
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Russia’s absent central bank chief and Palantir’s UK foes

item.title: German electricity grid equipment maker SGB-SMIT in early IPO talks
item.description: Company’s valuation could top €4bn as investors focus on AI and data centre boom

item.title: The tech giant mining Wall Street for AI cash
item.description: Former Goldman Sachs executive Dina Powell McCormick is helping Meta find ways to finance its AI ambitions

item.title: Iran’s ships head homeward to Gulf for ‘business as usual’ after US deal
item.description: Tracking data shows cargo vessels departing from Malaysian waters as Washington reaches agreement with Tehran

item.title: Blavatnik’s DAZN overhauls structure for potential new equity or IPO
item.description: Lossmaking sports streamer restructures business to make it easier to raise new money

item.title: Stockpickers: Castings, Tatton Asset Management, Boohoo Group
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Are insurers becoming dangerously addicted to private credit ratings?
item.description: We should worry about what happens when regulatory arbitrage goes unchecked

item.title: Netflix open to more deals with traditional TV
item.description: Chief executive Greg Peters said tie-up with France’s TF1 could be a model for the future, but denied bidding against Fox for Roku

item.title: AI is turning Nintendo and Sony products into accidental luxury goods
item.description: With component-makers busy supplying data centres, console prices are rising as demand outstrips production capacity

item.title: Inside Palantir’s fight over the future of the NHS
item.description: Software touted as the cure for chronic waiting lists in Britain’s public health service has become a sore point for the US tech group

item.title: PwC UK boss Amitrano wins bigger role in international shake-up
item.description: British head to speak for Europe and Middle East on global body as new Germany boss misses out

item.title: Tower Research muscles in on fast-growing fixed-income ETFs
item.description: New York-based trading firm has expanded quickly in funds investing in huge and fragmented debt market

item.title: Insurers seek to define ‘war’ as risk rises of global power clashes
item.description: Trade body considers updating Five Powers exclusion to incorporate hostile drone incursions and subsea attacks

item.title: ‘We created a monster’: companies rein in AI usage as costs strain budgets
item.description: Amazon, Walmart and Uber are among early adopters that have introduced caps or discouraged wasteful activity

item.title: From human urine to worm manure: unusual fertilisers boosted by Gulf conflict
item.description: Strait of Hormuz trade blockages fuel hunt for unconventional products that can be sourced locally

item.title: China wanted western tech. Now, the tables have turned. With John Minnich
item.description: Should the US and EU take a leaf out of China’s investment playbook?

item.title: A complicated World Cup for Iran’s diaspora
item.description: Iranian Americans are torn between supporting their football team and protesting Tehran’s regime

item.title: US launches trade investigation into Germany’s spending on new medicines
item.description: So-called Section 301 probe escalates Washington’s drug pricing war with Europe

item.title: Asics could run faster if it unshackled Onitsuka Tiger
item.description: Sportswear group is separating out its coolest brand but a sale or proper spin-off might be a better fit for shareholders

item.title: EU set to remove barriers to banks’ cross-border capital flows
item.description: Draft report by European Commission aims to boost performance of lenders in bloc compared to US rivals

item.title: SpaceX plots $20bn bond deal after record IPO
item.description: AI and rocket group is tapping debt markets after raising $86bn in stock market debut

item.title: SpaceX warns EU satellite plan risks undermining connectivity in Ukraine
item.description: Elon Musk’s group hits out at proposal by bloc to reserve part of spectrum band for European operators

item.title: UK government to overhaul ‘outdated’ home-selling process
item.description: Vendors will need to produce ‘sales packs’ containing information on the property’s condition prior to listing

item.title: Gatwick airport launches legal action after ministers relax runway slot rules
item.description: Regulation was intended to protect the airline industry from jet fuel shortages following the Iran crisis

item.title: Gulf oil shock speeds up India’s EV drive
item.description: Carmakers report increasing sales and showroom enquiries as fuel costs rise

item.title: US-Iran accord opens way for Hormuz charges, industry warns
item.description: Shipping executives fear language will enable Tehran to introduce fees after 60 days or a fund similar to Strait of Malacca

item.title: Accenture shares fall to lowest since 2017 as AI threat mounts
item.description: IT consultancy hit by concerns technology will hurt its business model

item.title: Rape convictions under review after UK detective allegedly used AI chatbot for paperwork
item.description: Derbyshire Police investigating whether officer used software to secure desired court outcome
